classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

metadata.author: The Canons of the Council in Trullo; Often Called The Quinisext Council

title: Canon XXXI.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: Clerics who in oratories which are in houses offer the Holy Mysteries or baptize, we decree ought to do this with the consent of the bishop of the place.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: Wherefore if any cleric shall not have so done, let him be deposed.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: Thou mayest not offer in an oratory in a private house without the consent of the bishop.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: On this whole subject the reader is referred to the curious and most interesting volume published by Venantius Monaldini of Venice, in 1765.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: I cannot better give its scope than by copying out its title in full.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: Commentarius Theologico-canonico-criticus De ecclesiis, earum reverentia, et asylo atque concordia sacerdotii, et imperii, auctore Josepho Aloysio Assemani.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: Josephi de Bonis, De Oratoriis Publicis; ac.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: Fortunati a Brixia De Oratoriis Domesticis, in supplementum celeberrimi operis Joannis Baptistæ Gattico De Oratoriis Domesticis, et usu altaris portatilis.
